paper_title: Mid-Pliocene Climate Modelled Using the UK Hadley Centre Model: PlioMIP Experiments 1 and 2

methods.description: This method involves using an alternative vegetation dataset to derive land surface properties for the control Experiment 1 simulation. The aim is to assess the impact of different vegetation data on climate model simulations.

steps.description: An alternative vegetation dataset was used to derive the land surface properties required by the model.
steps.searchable_summary: use alternative vegetation dataset to derive land surface properties

steps.description: The same lookup process as for the Pliocene data was applied to derive surface characteristics from biomes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply lookup process to derive surface characteristics from biomes

steps.description: Analyze differences due to the change in the control model, focusing on polar amplification and mean latitudinal temperature gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze model differences focusing on polar amplification and temperature gradient

steps.description: Compare predicted precipitation patterns between the atmosphere-only and coupled models.
steps.searchable_summary: compare precipitation patterns between atmosphere-only and coupled models

steps.description: Evaluate features of the PRISM3 data suggesting changes in ocean circulation, supported by coupled model results.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate ocean circulation changes using PRISM3 data and coupled model results

steps.description: Demonstrate sensitivity to boundary conditions using alternative datasets for both Pliocene and control simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: demonstrate sensitivity to boundary conditions using alternative datasets
